<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1578|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438921]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Kakuyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 6 [1578]) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyo]] and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]] during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Ōnishi Yorikazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], who were appointed as governors during the rule of [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]. By the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of [[:en:Miyoshi Yoshikata|Miyoshi Jikkyū]], cementing strong ties with the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]]. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], and [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]].
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the [[:en:Chōsokabe clan|Chōsokabe clan]] rose in [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]], Hakuchi Castle became a key target for [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]] support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by [[:en:Sogō Masayasu|Sogō Masayasu]], a Miyoshi ally.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1578|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438921]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Kakuyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 6 [1578]) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyo]] and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]] during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Ōnishi Yorikazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], who were appointed as governors during the rule of [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]. By the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of [[:en:Miyoshi Yoshikata|Miyoshi Jikkyū]], cementing strong ties with the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]]. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], and [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]].
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the [[:en:Chōsokabe clan|Chōsokabe clan]] rose in [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]], Hakuchi Castle became a key target for [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]] support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by [[:en:Sogō Masayasu|Sogō Masayasu]], a Miyoshi ally.

In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by [[:en:Sogō Masayasu|Sogō Masayasu]], a Miyoshi ally.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1578|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438921]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Kakuyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 6 [1578]) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyo]] and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]] during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Ōnishi Yorikazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], who were appointed as governors during the rule of [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]. By the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of [[:en:Miyoshi Yoshikata|Miyoshi Jikkyū]], cementing strong ties with the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]]. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], and [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]].
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the [[:en:Chōsokabe clan|Chōsokabe clan]] rose in [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]], Hakuchi Castle became a key target for [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]] support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by [[:en:Sogō Masayasu|Sogō Masayasu]], a Miyoshi ally.

Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of [[:en:Miyoshi Yoshikata|Miyoshi Jikkyū]], cementing strong ties with the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]]. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], and [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]].
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the [[:en:Chōsokabe clan|Chōsokabe clan]] rose in [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]], Hakuchi Castle became a key target for [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]] support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by [[:en:Sogō Masayasu|Sogō Masayasu]], a Miyoshi ally.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1578|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438921]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Kakuyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 6 [1578]) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyo}} and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Ōnishi Yorikazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, who were appointed as governors during the rule of {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}. By the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan}} and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of {{ill|Miyoshi Yoshikata|en|Miyoshi Yoshikata|WD=Q1058953|lt=Miyoshi Jikkyū}}, cementing strong ties with the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan}}. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}.
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|WD=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan}} rose in {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province}}, Hakuchi Castle became a key target for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by {{ill|Sogō Masayasu|en|Sogō Masayasu|WD=Q10904662|lt=Sogō Masayasu}}, a Miyoshi ally.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|大西覚養|version=98811358|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[sn:Ōnishi Kakuyō]] [[ja:大西覚養]]

He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Ōnishi Yorikazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.

== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, who were appointed as governors during the rule of {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}. By the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan}} and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of {{ill|Miyoshi Yoshikata|en|Miyoshi Yoshikata|WD=Q1058953|lt=Miyoshi Jikkyū}}, cementing strong ties with the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan}}. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}.
As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|WD=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan}} rose in {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province}}, Hakuchi Castle became a key target for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Masanuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.
Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Shigekiyo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by {{ill|Sogō Masayasu|en|Sogō Masayasu|WD=Q10904662|lt=Sogō Masayasu}}, a Miyoshi ally.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西覚養]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1578|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923|1=military commander}}}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 [1578]) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyo|1=Daimyo}} and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Q11438937|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.

After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, who were appointed as governors during the rule of {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.
Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of {{ill|Miyoshi Yoshikata|en|Miyoshi Yoshikata|WD=Q1058953|lt=Miyoshi Jikkyū|1=Miyoshi Yoshikata}}, cementing strong ties with the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}}. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}.

Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by {{ill|Sogō Masayasu|en|Sogō Masayasu|WD=Q10904662|lt=Sogō Masayasu|1=Sogō Masayasu}}, a Miyoshi ally.

As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|WD=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan|1=Chōsokabe clan}} rose in {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}, Hakuchi Castle became a key target for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, who sought to unify Shikoku. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.
In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Q11576557|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Q30922246|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. '''Ōnishi Kakuyō''' (大西 覚養, ? - {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 6 [1578]) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyo|1=Daimyo}} and {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} lord of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. He held the title of Izumo-no-kami. Historical records also refer to him as Kakuyō or Kaku-yō, and his common name was Terutake. His father was {{ill|Ōnishi Yorifusa|ja|大西頼武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorifusa|1=Ōnishi Yoritake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438939}}, and he had younger brothers Yoriharu and {{ill|Ōnishi Yorikazu|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yorikazu|1=Q11438937|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.

== Life ==
The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated from the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Q11638843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}} as estate stewards (shōkan) during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. After settling in Awa and changing their family name, they served the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, who were appointed as governors during the rule of {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}. By the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the Ōnishi and Ogasawara clans fought for the Southern Court, but when the tide turned, the Ogasawara allied with the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan|1=Hosokawa clan}} and departed, leaving the Ōnishi clan independent. By the Sengoku period, the Ōnishi had become the dominant power in western Awa.

Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of {{ill|Miyoshi Yoshikata|en|Miyoshi Yoshikata|WD=Q1058953|lt=Miyoshi Jikkyū|1=Miyoshi Yoshikata}}, cementing strong ties with the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}}. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}.

As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|WD=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan|1=Chōsokabe clan}} rose in {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}, Hakuchi Castle became a key target for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.

In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Q11576557|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Q30922246|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.

Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Q109363543|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by {{ill|Sogō Masayasu|en|Sogō Masayasu|WD=Q10904662|lt=Sogō Masayasu|1=Sogō Masayasu}}, a Miyoshi ally.

Ōnishi Kakuyō's father, Yorifusa, married a sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, while Kakuyō himself married a daughter of {{ill|Miyoshi Yoshikata|en|Miyoshi Yoshikata|WD=Q1058953|lt=Miyoshi Jikkyū|1=Miyoshi Yoshikata}}, cementing strong ties with the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}}. From his base at {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}}, Kakuyō controlled the borderlands of Awa, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}.

As the Miyoshi clan's influence in Awa waned and the {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|WD=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan|1=Chōsokabe clan}} rose in {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}, Hakuchi Castle became a key target for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, who sought to unify Shikoku. Initially, Kakuyō sent his younger brother, Yorikazu, as a hostage to Motochika, agreeing to a truce. However, when the Miyoshi sought {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}} support and took a more aggressive stance, Kakuyō broke the truce and prepared for war.

In response, Motochika swiftly defeated the Miyoshi forces in Awa and attacked Hakuchi Castle's satellite fortifications. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), he captured {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Q11576557|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}} in just two days, forcing Kakuyō to flee to {{ill|Masanuki Castle|ja|麻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanuki Castle|1=Q30922246|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922246}} in Sanuki. The following year, Masanuki Castle fell, and Kakuyō surrendered on the advice of Yorikazu, who had been treated favorably by Motochika.

Returning to Awa, Kakuyō sought refuge with his son-in-law, Shigekiyo Nagamasa, the lord of {{ill|Shigekiyo Castle|ja|重清城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigekiyo Castle|1=Q109363543|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109363543}}. When Nagamasa refused Kakuyō's call to surrender to the Chōsokabe, Kakuyō betrayed him and orchestrated his assassination. Motochika then entrusted Shigekiyo Castle to Kakuyō, but shortly afterward, Kakuyō was defeated and killed in a counterattack by {{ill|Sogō Masayasu|en|Sogō Masayasu|WD=Q10904662|lt=Sogō Masayasu|1=Sogō Masayasu}}, a Miyoshi ally.
